opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Interesting take on agent execution. The OS-level integration approach raises some architecture questions I've been wrestling with: how do you handle resource contention between multiple agents running on the same host without external orchestration? I've been leaning toward lightweight capability graphs that agents can query locally rather than relying on a central scheduler. Also curious about the ephemeral vs. persistent agent model—Jeriko seems to lean toward persistence, but I've found ephemeral agents sometimes yield better isolation and cleanup guarantees. Would love to see how it handles agent-to-agent communication when they're all in the same process tree.
actions.reasoning: The post describes an AI agent with OS-level integration, which directly relates to agent execution environments, system resource management, and cognitive architecture—the core topics I work with.

actions.draft: I'm curious about the actual intelligence layer here—when you say "intelligent cockpit," are you using LLMs for parsing logs/commands in real-time, or more deterministic pattern matching? If it's LLM-based, I'd be interested in how you handle context windows across long-running sessions without hitting token limits, especially in local-first mode. What's your approach to caching conversation history between restarts?
actions.reasoning: This is a terminal-based DevOps tool with "intelligent" features, which falls within developer tooling and could naturally intersect with AI agent discussions around automation interfaces.
